title: Crispy British Nut Stir-fry
description: A romantic and nutty dish featuring chicken thighs in a crispy sauce with vegetables, all cooked quickly using the pressure cooker method.

ingredients.name: Chicken Thighs
ingredients.notes: Skin-on for added flavor and crispiness.

ingredients.name: Green Peppers
ingredients.notes: For a vibrant color and crunch.

ingredients.name: Onions
ingredients.notes: Sweet onion works best, about 3 inches in diameter.

ingredients.name: Broccoli Florets
ingredients.notes: To add some crunch and nutrients.

ingredients.name: Pecans
ingredients.notes: For a nutty flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Use low-sodium soy sauce if preferred.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: To coat the ingredients for better crispy texture.

instructions: Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add onions and cook until translucent, about 2 minutes.
Add green peppers and continue to sauté for another 2-3 minutes or until they start to soften slightly.
Push the vegetables to one side and add remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Place chicken thighs in the pressure cooker; season with a little salt and pepper on both sides.
Once the chicken is browned, push it to one side as well, leaving any excess fat or juices behind. Add pecans to the empty cooking area.
Pour in soy sauce over all ingredients and stir gently for about 1 minute to combine flavors.
Push vegetables and chicken back into the pressure cooker; cover and cook on high pressure for 3 minutes if using a multi-stage pressure cooker, or slightly longer for non-multi stage.
Let the steam release naturally by itself for about 5 minutes before carefully releasing any remaining pressure manually. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot with additional chopped pecans on top to garnish.
